rulings.Case Title: JAMES TED HARVEY VS. ANDREW REBENNACK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants Daniel Maionchi And Dominic Maionchi; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Monday, Feb-23-2026, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF JAMES HARVEY'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants Daniel Maionchi And Dominic Maionchi; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants. Plaintiff James Ted Harvey's motion to compel depositions of Daniel Maionchi and Dominic Maionchi, and for sanctions, is continued for hearing to March 19, 2026. The parties are ordered to meet and confer telephonically or in person about deposition dates. The court has reviewed the parties' meet and confer correspondence. Harvey's motion recites meet and confer efforts through December 12. The Maionchis offered dates in correspondence that came after December 16. Harvey says he filed the motion on December 16, but the file-stamp date is December 24 and the proof of service date is December 24. On the record in the moving papers, the court is persuaded this is a scheduling issue and not misuse of discovery. Regardless, Harvey is entitled to the depositions. The court directs the parties to meet and confer within 10 days by telephone or in person to agree to mutually convenient deposition dates. The court continues the matter for hearing to March 19, 2026. The parties may file simultaneous supplemental briefs no later than five court days before the continued hearing date to apprise the court of any matters still in dispute. The court does not order sanctions for a misuse of the discovery process to date but will consider whether sanctions are appropriate based on meet and confer efforts through the continued hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CRISTIAN HERNANDEZ VS. PEOPLE CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Monday, Feb-23-2026, LINE 6. DEFENDANT PEOPLE CENTER, INC.'S, D/B/A RIPPLING, MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. The unopposed motion to compel arbitration of plaintiff Cristian Hernandez's individual claims is granted. The case is stayed pending arbitration, except that plaintiff may make a noticed motion to dismiss class claims and amend to assert PAGA penalty claims. The court observes that plaintiff has filed a declaration in support of a request to dismiss the class claims. Such a request must be made by noticed motion.
